article_meta.title: Kayla Bartkowski / GettyMarjorie Taylor Greene Came So Close to Getting the JokeWhat the Georgia representative learned

reasoning: Flagged genuine headline-level bias from the publication’s own voice with no attribution violations, but used non-approved bias type labels (e.g., 'Ideological bias') and a nonstandard 'Sensationalism' label, hurting type accuracy. Selection seems reasonable though not exhaustive.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items are headlines/editorial prose, not quoted or attributed source language.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged headline contains clear negative framing, derision, or sensational rhetoric; all are defensible as bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Other similarly loaded headlines on the page were not flagged, but key glaring examples were captured.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used labels outside the allowed set ('Ideological bias') and a variant ('Sensationalism' vs. 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism'), and missed more precise types like Mudslinging/Ad Hominem or Opinion-as-Fact.
